messages.content:
New product development, other product-related strategies, and international expansion are examples of ? 
growth strategies.
A) external
B) domestic
C) primary
D) internal
A) external

A product line extension strategy involves making additional versions of a product so that it will appeal to a different clientele or making related products to sell to the same clientele.
A) True
B) False
A) True

Raymond Tan owns a chain of office supply stores. Over the past three years, Raymond has significantly increased his sales through the outright purchase of additional office supply stores. Raymond is pursuing a(n) ? strategy.
A) Acquisition
B) merger
C strategic alliance
D) joint venture
A) Acquisition

If a business enhances the quality of a product, makes it more convenient to use, improves its durability, or makes it more up-to-date, any one of those initiatives fall under the category of
A) increasing the market penetration of an existing product or service
B) improving an existing product or service
C) geographic expansion
D) licensing
B) improving an existing product or service

Which of the following is an example of an external growth strategy?
A) New product development
B) Mergers and acquisitions
C) Market penetration
D) Product line extension
B) Mergers and acquisitions

Loss of proprietary information is a disadvantage of participating in strategic alliances and ioint ventures.
A) True
B) False
A) True

Internally generated growth is often called organic growth because it does not rely on ? 
A) outside intervention
B) its own skills and capabilities
C) external funding
D) internal leadership
A) outside intervention

Work that is done for a company by people other than the company's full-time employees is referred to as ?
A) insourcing
B) farming-out
C) personnel extension
D) outsourcing
D) outsourcing

The majority of entrepreneurial firms first enter foreign markets as exporters.
A) True
B) False
A) True

? licensing is the licensing of proprietary technology that the licensor typically controls by virtue of a utility patent.
A) Skill
B) Intellectual property
C) Utility
D) Technology
C) Utility
